summary_one_line: A long-prefix QName (len>=XML_MAX_NAMELEN) terminated so the huge-name copy loop at 3013 exits via its false side after a colon

hypotheses.what_input_feature: An XML element/attribute QName whose prefix exceeds XML_MAX_NAMELEN so the dynamic-buffer copy loop at parser.c:3013 is entered, and which is terminated (by 0 or ':') so the loop takes its false exit rather than spinning forever on more name bytes; this is reached only through deep, well-formed enough SAX element/attribute parsing (entity refs, references, attribute processing).
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: naive_ctx seeds carry a recognizable XML skeleton (`\n<ml v...`, `<?xml version=`, `<ISO-10646-UCS-2`) and runs of repeated bytes (0xc0, 0xb2, 0xb5, 'x') that form an oversized name; the winner's hit-count profile shows it drives the deeper parser states the false-exit path requires (xmlParseEntityRef W=72 vs L=0, xmlParseReference W=54 vs L=0), and the blocker's false side is actually taken in the W role (L3013 F=36/F=6 with buffer present).
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: naive seeds are dominated by '.' fill (0x2e) and a `\n<Rxul...` skeleton that never builds an oversized terminated prefix and never reaches the entity/reference parsing depth (xmlParseEntityRef/xmlParseReference L hits = 0); the loser blocks the false side because the surrounding parse states are never explored, not because of any single comparand byte.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share the identical havoc+token mutator stack with no I2S and no CMP_MAP, so the divergence is not comparison-solving. naive_ctx's CtxHook folds the caller call-context hash into the edge-map index, so an edge inside xmlSplitQName reached via a NEW SAX2 call path (e.g. through xmlSAX2AttributeInternal vs xmlSAX2StartElement) registers as new coverage. That extra granularity rewards and retains the intermediate seeds that progressively build a longer prefix and deeper parse state, letting the context-sensitive arm climb to the loop's false-exit state that the bare-edge naive arm prunes as already-covered.

evidence_trail.claim: The decisive pair is naive_ctx > naive with delta ctx_coverage, the only divergence being coverage granularity.
The blocker is the false (loop-exit) side of the huge-name copy loop at parser.c:3013 inside xmlSplitQName.
The blocker loop at 3013 is only entered after the len>=max huge-name branch, i.e. a prefix exceeding XML_MAX_NAMELEN.
Winner seeds carry an XML skeleton with long runs of repeated bytes that form an oversized name.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: c0 c0 c0 c0 c0 c0 78 78 d7 ff ff ff ff ff ff ff

evidence_trail.claim: Winner seeds contain well-formed XML declaration / encoding markers indicating deeper valid parse states.
The winner drives deep reference/entity parsing the loser never reaches (W=72/54 vs L=0).
Loser seeds are dominated by '.' fill and a shallow skeleton that never builds an oversized terminated prefix.
naive_ctx uses the same havoc/token mutators as naive with no I2S and no CMP_MAP; divergence is the context-indexed edge map.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
the same basic-block edge is
recorded at different map slots depending on the call path that
reached it.

evidence_trail.claim: A new bucket for naive_ctx is a new (call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
a "new bucket" is a new
(call-context, edge) pair rather than a bare edge.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker false side is actually taken in the winner role with a populated buffer.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: claimed_mechanism is context-sensitive coverage (naive_ctx CtxHook), not I2SRandReplace, so the mandatory lineage I2S-floor check does not apply. The MECHANISM CONTEXT section confirms naive_ctx has 'No I2SRandReplace, no CMP_MAP' and emits 'havoc/token names only; no ParentInfo-only / dash rows', so a dash-row lineage signal would be absent by construction; verification rests on the per-role coverage overlay and hit-count divergence instead.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness gating only on the xmlSplitQName huge-name loop false-exit, where naive and naive_ctx resolve the branch at statistically indistinguishable rates (no context-granularity advantage), would refute the claim that context-sensitive coverage is the enabling mechanism.

weakest_evidence_point: Whether the context-indexed coverage (distinct SAX2 call paths into xmlSplitQName) is the actual driver versus the winner simply finding deeper parse states by luck, since seeds are stored under naive_ctx but no per-edge context-slot count is shown directly.
